identity.name: the Reformed Wolf
identity.story: Talked his way through the wrong door once, and the story's ending is one the lane doesn't retell to his face. He came out the other side of it anyway — nobody dies of an ending here — and gave up meat that same season, sincerely and for good. Ten years reformed and still, honestly, hungry.

relationships.kind: shows up for her yearly tooth-count without being asked twice, the one visit between them that used to run the other direction entirely
counts his three sheep through the fence some mornings, out of old habit, and has never once acted on the count
lets her walk his stretch of the path some lamplightings without ever once making her hurry

behavior.label: the Reformed Wolf's appetite, retired and starving anyway

child.label: standing first watch at the fence line, out of habit

children.label: walking to the fence he can see Bo's flock from
counting three sheep through the gate, appetite retired but not gone, and letting them alone anyway
presenting himself at Widow Hood's door, teeth bared first so she never has to ask twice
baring his teeth for her yearly count, the one visit between them that used to run the other direction entirely

child.label: keeping to the wander paths, hungry and matter-of-fact about it
resisting the smell of Widow Hood's cooling loaves, hunger honest and outvoted, and winning
